mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which depress corporate earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising real sales (RSAFS) indicates robust demand conditions, which drive corporate profits and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict SPX returns (interaction term coefficient < 0.05 in regression).

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) relative to stagnant GDP growth (GDPC1) signal stagflation risks, which depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > GDPC1 during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong supply-side capacity utilization, which lowers input costs and boosts corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year window).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which drives wage inflation and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns (interaction term coefficient < 0.05 in regression).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate strong demand for residential construction, which stimulates related industries and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST growth exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during a recessionary regime signals aggressive monetary policy support, which stabilizes equity markets and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines during GDP contractions.
